name: Makapu'u Lighthouse Trail
tagline: Fully paved and stroller-friendly with whale sightings Nov–May and dramatic ocean cliffs.
parking: Free lot off Kalanianaole Hwy (fills by 9am weekends)
hours: Sunrise to sunset daily (parking lot gate opens at sunrise)
bestTime: Jan–Mar for peak whale watching; early morning to avoid heat and crowds
talkStory: Bring binoculars — humpback whales breach offshore Jan through Mar and you can spot them from multiple viewpoints along the trail. Fully paved and wheelchair/stroller accessible. About 2 miles round trip. No shade, so bring water and sunscreen. The tidepools below the lighthouse are worth the extra scramble down if conditions are calm.
